title: marsdenia suberosa
intro: Marsdenia suberosa, also known as the barkless milkvine, is a woody vine in the Apocynaceae family native to the tropical forests of Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of Marsdenia suberosa include its ability to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential in reducing inflammation and improving immune function. Herbal preparations of Marsdenia suberosa include extracts and infusions used in traditional medicine, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and potential uses.
